mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration that triggers immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where the unemployment threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust consumer demand that sustains corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to produce a positive Sharpe ratio on 60-day forward returns over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 2.0% annualized during a post-recession recovery phase confirms capacity utilization improvements that boost equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative or flat when industrial production growth exceeds the threshold during the specified recovery regime.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening economic activity that supports corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Monthly payroll increases consistently coincide with negative 5-day forward returns across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index reading above 2.5% triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening that compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in over 60% of periods where inflation surpasses the 2.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising housing starts and falling retail sales indicates a sector rotation away from consumer discretionary stocks, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The specified divergence pattern yields a positive information coefficient on 20-day forward returns over a 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals supply-side constraints that outweigh demand support, leading to negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines during tightening periods are followed by positive 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of observed cases.
